mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC for FEDFUNDS against 20-day forward returns falls below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window, or positive returns exceed negative returns in >65% of high-rate periods.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions and rising recession probability, triggering immediate negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE increases by >0.2 percentage points month-over-month
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in >60% of instances where UNRATE rises >0.2pp, or the signal loses statistical significance (p>0.05) in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates synchronized consumer and capital demand growth, which drives corporate revenue expectations higher and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to generate a positive mean return differential (>0.5% per month) compared to baseline, or the interaction term loses significance in a multivariate regression over a 5-year walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production reflects strengthening supply-side capacity utilization and manufacturing output, which generates positive short-term S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days through momentum and earnings revision channels.
failure_condition: INDPRO acceleration fails to predict 5-day forward returns with a Rank IC > 0.01, or the signal reverses direction during periods of supply chain disruption.

mechanism_narrative: When consumer price inflation exceeds a 3.5% annualized threshold, markets price in aggressive monetary tightening and margin compression, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in >55% of months where CPIAUCSL YoY > 3.5%, or the threshold effect disappears when controlling for FEDFUNDS changes.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in total nonfarm payrolls and a declining unemployment rate confirms robust labor market absorption, supporting consumer spending and corporate profitability to drive positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined PAYEMS and UNRATE signal yields a negative mean forward return, or the interaction effect is statistically indistinguishable from zero in a 10-year out-of-sample evaluation.

mechanism_narrative: Higher quarterly real GDP growth directly correlates with stronger aggregate corporate earnings and economic momentum, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days through fundamental valuation channels.
failure_condition: GDPC1 shows no statistically significant correlation (p>0.10) with 60-day forward returns over a 15-year rolling window, or the signal consistently underperforms a buy-and-hold baseline.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous decline in the federal funds rate and consumer price inflation signals a successful soft landing and reduced financial stress, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS declines while CPIAUCSL simultaneously declines
failure_condition: The dual-decline regime produces negative mean forward returns, or the signal fails to outperform a market-neutral benchmark in >70% of historical easing episodes.
